reasoning: Ian Nepomniachtchi's FIDE classical rating on the Wikipedia page as of 2025-11-09 is 2732, because Wikipedia uses the official FIDE monthly ratings published on the 1st of each month. A 1% increase would require a new rating of over 2759.32. However, FIDE rankings do not update mid-month, meaning on the first resolution date (2025-11-16), a 1% increase is effectively impossible (the page will still show 2732). Furthermore, he was just eliminated from the FIDE World Cup in early November 2025 by a much lower-rated player (Diptayan Ghosh, 2573). This devastating loss will heavily penalize his rating, dropping it to around 2723 for the December list, bumping him out of the Top 20 altogether (and off the Wikipedia page's Top 20 table). Regaining nearly 40 classical rating points by February 2026 to cross 2760 is statistically unfeasible for a player of his age and current form, especially given a lack of high-profile tournaments to score such massive points. Therefore, the probability of him achieving this 1% rating bump is exceptionally low across all horizons.
